title: How health coverage affects your 2020 federal income tax return
meta-title: Your 2020 Federal Income Tax Return & Marketplace Health Coverage
meta-description: Learn how your healthcare insurance will impact your 2020 federal tax filing. What you need to know for maximum return, premium tax credit, and more.
page_name: How health coverage affects your 2020 federal income tax return
excerpt: Learn how your healthcare insurance will impact your 2020 federal tax filing. What you need to know for maximum return, premium tax credit, and more.
content:
If you haven’t filed your 2020 taxes, or filed but failed to “reconcile” your premium tax credit, use the information on these pages. Some links may point to IRS information about filing this year’s taxes, so read any IRS pages carefully.

Get information about filing taxes for this year.

You should’ve already filed your 2020 taxes, but if you haven’t, you should do so immediately. When you file your taxes for the 2020 tax filing year:

  • You’ll provide additional information when you file your 2020 federal income tax return.
  • You may have to complete one or two new tax forms.
  • You may have to use a tax tool to find 2020 Silver premiums to complete your tax return.
  • Unlike in past tax years, if you didn’t have coverage during 2020, the fee no longer applies. This means you don’t need an exemption in order to avoid the penalty.

Your 2020 health coverage status

If you haven’t filed 2020 taxes, find the scenario that best applied to your health status in 2020 to get your next steps:

  • If you had a 2020 Marketplace plan with premium tax credits
  • If you had a 2020 Marketplace plan without premium tax credits
  • If you had 2020 job-based health coverage
  • If you had other health coverage for 2020
  • If you didn’t have health coverage
